publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07669Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation (GLAAD) records7669Compiled by Brenda MarstonDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
November 21, 20012B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humsxThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:03:44 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation (GLAAD) records
Gay & Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation
7669
68 cubic feet. 68 cubic feet.
1985-2001. Administrative files, correspondence, newspaper clippings. Administrative and subject files, correspondence, media kits, press releases, clippings, and files on the GLAAD Media Award events from the New York City, Atlanta, and Los Angeles offices. Collection material in English 4031673
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Brenda Marston
Date completed:
July 6, 2001
EAD encoding:
Peter M. Martinez, November 2001Sara McDermott, June 2009
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, December 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
GLAAD works to ensure fair portrayal of lesbians and gays in all forms of media, from newspapers to major motion pictures.
SERIES LIST
Series I. New York Office Administrative Files
Boxes 1-7
Series II. New York Office Media Alerts, Press Releases, and Clippings
Boxes 8-11,20-22, 23-67
Series III. Atlanta Office Administrative Files
Boxes 12-14
Series IV. Atlanta Office Subject and Media Files
Boxes 15-19
Series V. Los Angeles Office Subject and Media Files
Box 22
SEPARATED MATERIAL: Includes comic books from the "Comics, Cartoons" files in Series II. New York Office Media Alerts, Press Releases, and Clippings.
Tales of the Closet, published by the Hetrick-Martin Institute, v.1 nos.1-5, 1987-1990
Seven Miles A Second by David Wojnarowicz and James Romberger, 1996
Love and Rockets #50, Fantagraphics Books, 1996
Death. Gaiman, Neil, et. al. DC Comics: 1994
Real War Stories: Citizen Soldier, Eclipse Books, 1991
Metropolis S.C.U.: Special Crimes Unit, DC Comics, 4 issue mini-series, nos. 1-4, Nov.1994-Feb.1995
Northstar, Marvel Comics, v.1, nos.3-4 of 4, June-July 1994
Chiaroscuro: The Private Lives of Leonardo Da Vinci, DC Comics, Nos. 1-9, July 1995-Mar.1996
Quarantines and Death: The Far Right's Homophobic Agenda, by Mab Segrest and Leonard Zeskind, published by Center for Democratic Renewal and Education, 1991
This Week in Texas, Volume 20, Number 29, September 23-29, 1994, published by Texas Weekly Times Newspaper Company
World: The Journal of the Unitarian Universalist Association, Volume 8, Number 4, July/August 1994
World: The Journal of the Unitarian Universalist Association, Volume 8, Number 5, September/October 1994
A Magazine: The Asian American Quarterly, Volume 2, Number 3, Fall 1993
The Advocate, Issue 711, July 9, 1996
Out, Number 15, September 1994
Southern Forum, Volume 2, Number 13, July 1, 1994
Alternatives: National Lesbian and Gay Journalists Association, Volume 4, Number 3, Summer 1994
1994 Atlanta Lesbian and Gay Pride Official Commemorative Program, June 1994, Official Commemorative Program for the 1994 Atlanta Pride Celebration, published by Southern Voice and The Atlanta Lesbian and Gay Pride Committee
Cite As:
Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation (GLAAD) records, #7669. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Administrative and subject files, correspondence, media kits, press releases, clippings, and files on the GLAAD Media Award events from the New York City, Atlanta, and Los Angeles offices.
Homosexuality in motion pictures -- United States. Homosexuality on television -- United States. Gay rights -- United States.
